build_system: Gradle multi-module build. Run `./gradlew build` to build all modules, `./gradlew test` to run tests, and `./gradlew spotlessApply` to enforce code formatting (Spotless with Google Java Format 1.34.1). Shell and MCP modules require Java 25+; parser and tools modules target Java 8 bytecode. The `jafar-gradle-plugin` is wired as a composite build — no `publishToMavenLocal` is needed during development. After changing `settings.gradle`, run `./gradlew --stop` and `rm -rf demo/.gradle/` to clear caches. A pre-commit hook at `.githooks/pre-commit` runs `spotlessApply` and restages; activate it with `git config core.hooksPath .githooks`.
test_infrastructure: JUnit Jupiter 5 with Mockito. Place tests under `src/test/java` mirroring package paths, naming them `*Test.java`. Run all tests with `./gradlew test`; run a specific class with `./gradlew :module:test --tests "FullyQualifiedTest"`. CI runs a matrix on JDK 8 and JDK 21 for every PR. JFR test recordings live in `src/test/resources/`; binary resources must be fetched first with `./get_resources.sh` (Git LFS). On JDK 21, japicmp validates API compatibility — breaking changes require a major version bump. The `check` task depends on `spotlessCheck`, so formatting failures block the build.
sharp_edges: Never add a developer-local `org.gradle.java.home` to `gradle.properties` — it breaks builds on other machines. The plugin catalog `jfr-shell-plugins.json` must always contain the latest released version (never SNAPSHOT); the catalog version must not be downgraded across major/minor boundaries. When modifying the JFR parser, check both `parser-core/src/main/java/.../TypeGenerator.java` and `parser-core/src/java21/java/.../TypeGenerator.java` — the dual-version implementation must stay in sync. Test binary resources require `./get_resources.sh` before the first build. Do not commit recordings outside Git LFS.
pr_ci_rules: CI (pr-tests.yml) runs on every PR with a JDK 8 × JDK 21 matrix; both must pass. Run `./gradlew test shadowJar` locally before opening a PR. Spotless formatting is enforced — run `./gradlew spotlessApply` and commit the result. japicmp runs on JDK 21 and fails the build on breaking shell-core API changes. Release is tag-driven (`v*`) and publishes to Maven Central, GitHub Packages, and the JBang catalog automatically; do not push version tags to main without first updating `build.gradle`, `jafar-gradle-plugin/build.gradle`, and `CHANGELOG.md`.
file_layout: Multi-module Gradle project rooted at `/`. Core parser: `parser-core/`, `parser-codegen/`, `parser/` (aggregate). Annotation processor: `jafar-processor/`. Shared shell abstractions: `shell-core/`. Interactive CLIs: `jfr-shell/` (JFR), `hdump-shell/` (heap dump), `pprof-shell/` (pprof), `otlp-shell/` (OTLP), `jafar-shell/` (unified launcher). Backend plugins: `jfr-shell-jafar/`, `jfr-shell-jdk/`; TCK: `jfr-shell-tck/`. MCP server: `jfr-mcp/`. Gradle plugin: `jafar-gradle-plugin/` (separate included build). Demo: `demo/` (separate build). Corpus and KG artefacts: `.sphinx/`.
